myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, before the mountains had voices, there lived a solitary wind spirit named Aella. She roamed the jagged peaks of the highest ranges, silent and unseen. One day, a wandering shepherd named Caelen became lost in her snowy domain. He played a wooden flute to comfort his flock, and Aella was enchanted by the melody. She wished to speak to him, to guide him home, but spirits were forbidden by the Sky King from speaking directly to mortals.

If she broke the ancient law, she would lose her form forever. Seeing Caelen shivering as the freezing night fell, Aella finally made her fateful choice. She called out his name, her voice ringing clear across the deep valleys. "Caelen! This way!" The shepherd followed her voice to safety.

Instantly, the Sky King’s wrath descended. Aella was stripped of her body, reduced to nothing but a lingering presence bound to the stone. Yet, the Sky King could not erase her voice entirely. Now, whenever a traveler calls out among the rocky cliffs, Aella answers. She cannot speak her own thoughts, but she eagerly repeats the words of mortals, a fading memory of the day she chose compassion over her own life.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, before the sun learned to scorch the earth, the sky was ruled by a gentle spirit named Aella. Aella possessed a voice that commanded the winds, but she was lonely, floating far above the world of men. One twilight, she saw a mortal shepherd, Elian, playing a wooden flute on a high mountain peak. The melody was so sorrowful and sweet that Aella descended, wrapping herself in a cloak of silver mist.

They fell in love, meeting only at the edge of dawn and dusk. But the Sun God, jealous of the mortal’s beautiful music, decreed that no spirit could ever touch the earth. He banished Aella to the highest stratosphere, where the air was too thin to carry her voice or Elian’s music. Heartbroken, Aella could do nothing but watch Elian grow old alone.

Unable to hold him, Aella began to weep every morning just before the Sun God awoke. Her tears fell through the atmosphere, landing softly on the grass, the leaves, and the flowers of the world. Today, we call these tears the morning dew—a quiet, glittering reminder of a love separated by the sky, forever trying to touch the earth.

As the flock weakened and Elian collapsed from the blazing heat, Aella could not stand by. Defying the Sun God’s ancient laws, she threw herself from the highest stratosphere, sacrificing her immortal form to envelop the valley in a cool, life-saving mist. 

Furious, the Sun God shattered her physical body, ensuring she could never hold Elian again. Yet, love proved stronger than the sun's wrath. Though invisible, Aella’s spirit survived, transforming into the gentle morning breeze. Today, just before the sun's heat grows fierce, Aella stirs. She dances through the valleys, brushing against the grass and carrying the sweet scent of blooming flowers—a perpetual, refreshing sigh from a spirit who gave everything to protect the mortal she loved.

Myth: Long before the seasons turned bitter, Aella was a spirit of the autumn mists. She spent her days drifting among the high peaks, entranced by the sweet, sorrowful melodies of a mortal shepherd named Caelen. Though she longed to dance to his wooden flute, the Sky King had strictly forbidden spirits from interfering with the mortal realm.

One year, a terrible, biting wind swept down from the stars, threatening to freeze Caelen and his flock. Unable to watch him perish, Aella defied the ancient law. She descended from the peaks and wrapped her thick, silver mist tightly around the valley, creating a blanket to shield the shepherd from the deadly chill.

The Sky King saw her disobedience and struck with his icy wrath. He froze Aella’s misty form, trapping her forever on the blades of grass and the leaves of the trees. Caelen survived the night, waking to find the world covered in delicate, glittering white crystals. To this day, when the autumn turns cold, Aella returns as the early frost—a beautiful, silent guardian bound to the earth, forever protecting the world of the shepherd she loved.
